feedback.text: Consumers rights in the digital market have always felt undermined. Video games have especially felt it, when companies decide on a whim when to cut off all support and remove the game that people have already paid for. Software companies have tried to make older versions of their software unusable, then market a new version where you have to pay for them again. It's time for some changes in the digital market, where consumers aren't exploited and are treated fairly with stronger digital consumer rights
